mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production index reflect accelerating manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, which translate into higher expected corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return is non-positive following months with INDPRO growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25 percent signals an exhausted conventional monetary toolkit and acute economic slack, leading equity investors to price persistent earnings weakness, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS < 0.25 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate indicate a tightening labor market that boosts wage income and consumer spending, jointly driving positive S&P 500 returns; neither signal alone suffices because hiring without unemployment improvement reflects labor-force-entry noise.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases and UNRATE decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive during joint month-over-month PAYEMS increase and UNRATE decrease episodes over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime characterized by near-zero policy rates, month-over-month retail sales growth signals demand normalization that lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS changes conditioned on FEDFUNDS < 0.25 falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.5 million units indicate robust construction activity and residential investment momentum that spills over into broader economic demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative on average when HOUST > 1500 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with stagnant or falling industrial production create a stagflationary gap where input costs squeeze margins without offsetting revenue growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns; the interaction captures margin compression that isolated series miss.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases and INDPRO decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative during simultaneous month-over-month CPIAUCSL increase and INDPRO decrease over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 6 percent signals excess labor slack that suppresses wage growth and household demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations reset lower.
failure_condition: Average 60-day forward return is non-negative when UNRATE > 6.0 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth confirms the economy's trajectory and drives positive short-term S&P 500 returns as investors revise earnings expectations upward in response to output expansion.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive GDPC1 surprises are non-positive over a 2-year rolling window.
